column2: A piece of electrinic device such as a keyboard, speaker, or smartphone.
MS PowerPoint is used to create this.
MS Excel is used to create this.
MS Access is used to create this.
Keyboard and mouse are examples of this type of device, but printer and monitor are not.
Printer and monitor are examples of this type of device, but keyboard and mouse are not.
HDDs and flash drives are examples of this, but RAM and ROM are not.
HDD stands for Hard Disk D____.
Today, SSDs (S____ State Drives) are taking over HDDs. Unlike HDDs, SSDs do not have a spinning disk and are faster.
A flash drive is useful because it is _______.
ROM stands for R___ Only Memory.
RAM stands for R____ Access Memory.
RAM is better than ROM because it is rewritable, but worse because it is v______ and losses data without power.
Memory directly connected to the CPU is called what?
Storage is also known as _________ memory.
Storage is better than memory in capacity, but worse in the s____.
CPU stands for Central P_________ Unit.
The CPU is described as the _____ of a computer.
UI stands for User I______.
The "G" in GUI stands for G_______. A GUI makes computers easier to use.
GUI took over CUI (Character User Interface). CUI accepted input from a keyboard, but not from a _____.
This is also known as QWERTY.
Bold, Save and Print buttons in MS Word are examples of these in a GUI.
File, Home, and Insert at the top of the GUI in MS Word are examples of these.
When two documents are open in the GUI of MS Word, they appear in separate _______.
The arrow you move in a GUI with a mouse.
A row of icons in the GUI of MS Word, such as Bold and Italic.
A document or piece of data stored on a computer.
A container used to organize files.
______ keys in a keyboard are used to type letters and numbers such as A-Z and 0-9
________ keys in a keyboard are labeled F1 to F12 and perform special commands.
_______ keys in a keyboard include Ctrl, Alt, and Shift and are used with other keys.
________ keys in a keyboard include Home, End, Insert, and Delete and are used when editing text.
The number pad on the right side of a keyboard is called the _______ keypad.
The file extension for a plain text file.
The file extension for a MS Word file.
The file extension for a MS Excel file.
The file extension for a MS PowerPoint file.
A file format used for documents that keep the same layout when shared.
A common file extension for digital photos.
An image file format that supports transparent backgrounds.
An extension is the part of a filename after this.
The higest directory in a file system, above all other folders.
The top folder of a project, often located directly under the root directory.
A: and B: were used for ______ disks, so the Windows root directory is often C:.
Unix uses slash (/) to separate folders in a path. Windows uses what?
The vertical arrangement of cells in a spreadsheet.
The horizontal arrangement of cells in a spreadsheet.
The smallest unit of a spreadsheet where numbers or text are stored
